skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance 2.0, HappyHorse, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance 2.0, HappyHorse 1.0, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman, Fabric, HunyuanVideo. Capabilities: text-to-video, image-to-video, reference-to-video, video editing, lipsync, avatar animation, video upscaling, foley sound. Use for: social media videos, marketing content, explainer videos, product demos, AI avatars. Triggers: video generation, ai video, text to video, image to video, veo, animate image, video from image, ai animation, video generator, generate video, t2v, i2v, ai video maker, create video with ai, runway alternative, pika alternative, sora alternative, kling alternative, seedance, happyhorse
skill.summary: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance 2.0, HappyHorse, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference...
skill.author: inference-sh-skills

security_audit.summary: The skill is a legitimate AI video generation tool using the inference.sh CLI (belt). Static findings flagged 59 external_command locations and 19 network locations, but evaluation confirms all are FALSE POSITIVES. The backtick syntax in SKILL.md markdown code blocks is documentation only (not executable Ruby), and hardcoded URLs reference legitimate service documentation and user-provided media URLs. No command injection vectors exist. The skill only invokes the belt CLI with hardcoded app IDs and JSON parameters, no user input is interpolated into shell commands.

content.user_title: Generate AI Videos with Google Veo and 40+ Models
content.value_statement: Creating professional videos from text prompts or images is time-consuming and requires technical skills. This skill provides a simple CLI interface to generate, edit, and enhance AI videos using state-of-the-art models like Google Veo, Seedance 2.0, and HappyHorse directly from Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Generate videos from text prompts using 40+ AI models including Google Veo 3.1, Seedance 2.0, and HappyHorse
Animate static images into videos using Wan 2.5, Seedance 2.0, and HappyHorse image-to-video models
Create talking head videos with lipsync using OmniHuman, Fabric, and PixVerse
Edit existing videos with natural language prompts using HappyHorse Video Edit
Add sound effects, upsample video quality, and merge multiple videos
Generate synchronized audio alongside videos using supported audio generation models
limitations: Requires inference.sh account and CLI installation (belt)
Video generation is asynchronous - results return via streaming/polling
Model quality and availability depends on inference.sh service
Generation costs vary by model and are controlled by inference.sh pricing

use_cases.title: Create Marketing Videos from Product Descriptions
use_cases.description: Generate promotional videos for social media by describing the scene in natural language. Describe a product in detail and have an AI model render a professional-looking video.

use_cases.title: Animate Illustrations for Explainers
use_cases.description: Take existing artwork or stock images and animate them into engaging explainer content. Use image-to-video models to bring static visuals to life with realistic motion.

use_cases.title: Produce AI Avatar Presentations
use_cases.description: Create talking head videos where a portrait image lip-syncs to provided audio. Generate the audio separately with text-to-speech, then animate the speaker.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Text-to-Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-video-generation skill to generate a video with Google Veo 3.1 Fast. Create a [SCENE DESCRIPTION] video.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a basic video from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-Video Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-video-generation skill to animate the image at [IMAGE_URL]. Create a video showing [MOTION DESCRIPTION].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have a specific image to animate

prompt_templates.title: Video with Natural Language Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-video-generation skill to edit the video at [VIDEO_URL]. Change [EDIT DESCRIPTION].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to modify an existing video

prompt_templates.title: Avatar Lipsync Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-video-generation skill to create a talking head video. Use the image at [PORTRAIT_URL] and audio from [AUDIO_URL].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a presenter-style video with lip sync

output_examples.input: Generate a drone shot video flying over a mountain range at sunset
output_examples.output: A 5-second AI-generated video showing camera footage of mountains with warm sunset lighting, generated using Google Veo 3.1 Fast via the inference.sh belt CLI

output_examples.input: Animate my product photo showing it rotating slowly
output_examples.output: An animated video of the product rotating with natural lighting and subtle reflections, created using Wan 2.5 image-to-video model

best_practices: Be specific with visual details - include lighting, camera angle, and motion descriptions for better results
Use natural language video editing to iterate on existing videos rather than regenerating from scratch
Test different models for your use case - Seedance 2.0 offers synchronized audio while HappyHorse provides physical realism
anti_patterns: Do not include special characters or shell metacharacters in prompt text passed to belt commands
Do not assume generation completes immediately - always handle async streaming or polling for results
Do not use placeholder URLs that are not publicly accessible - models need to download input images and videos

faq.question: How do I get started with this skill?
faq.answer: Install the inference.sh CLI (belt) by following the install instructions at raw.githubusercontent.com/inference-sh/skills/cli-install.md. Then run belt login to authenticate with your inference.sh account.

faq.question: Which model should I use for text-to-video?
faq.answer: Google Veo 3.1 offers the best quality with frame interpolation. For faster results, use Veo 3.1 Fast. Seedance 2.0 adds synchronized audio generation. HappyHorse excels at physically realistic motion.

faq.question: Can I generate videos with audio?
faq.answer: Yes. Several models support audio: Veo 3.1, Veo 3, and Seedance 2.0 can generate videos with synchronized audio. You can also add foley sound effects using the HunyuanVideo Foley tool.

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: Generation time varies by model and video length. Fast models like Veo 3.1 Fast complete in under a minute. Full quality models may take several minutes. Use belt app run with streaming to monitor progress.

faq.question: Can I edit videos I already have?
faq.answer: Yes. HappyHorse Video Edit lets you modify existing videos using natural language prompts. Examples include changing backgrounds, adjusting lighting, or extending scenes.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: Most models accept standard image formats like JPG, PNG, and WebP via URL. Provide publicly accessible URLs - models download input images for processing.
